text:
1. Stock Reduction Rule (Atomic Inventory Management) The Stock Reduction rule acts as the primary mechanism for maintaining data integrity during transactions. In a multi-agent environment, "race conditions" can occur if two agents attempt to buy the last available book simultaneously. By implementing this logic as a SWRL rule within the ontology layer, the system ensures atomicity.

Logic Flow: The rule listens for the creation of an instance of the Order class. Once an order is finalized (status set to "Completed"), the rule immediately retrieves the associated Book individual and its current hasStockQuantity.

Execution: It performs a mathematical subtraction where the New Stock equals the Current Stock minus the Order Quantity.

Impact: This update happens at the knowledge base level before any other agent can query the stock, effectively preventing overselling and ensuring the inventory count always reflects real-time physical stock.

2. Low Stock Alert Rule (Proactive Monitoring) This rule implements a continuous monitoring system that governs the supply chain logic. Instead of requiring Employee agents to manually check every shelf (which is computationally expensive), this rule automatically flags items that need attention.

Logic Flow: The rule constantly compares a book's hasStockQuantity against its defined reorderLevel (e.g., 5 units).

Trigger Condition: If the Stock Quantity is less than or equal to the Reorder Level, the rule infers a new property value: it tags the specific inventory individual with a status of needsRestock.

Impact: This shifts the behavior of Employee agents from "polling" (constantly checking) to "event-driven" (reacting only when an alert exists), significantly optimizing agent performance and CPU usage.

3. Auto Restock Rule (Self-Healing Supply Chain) The Auto Restock rule simulates an automated supply chain or warehouse replenishment system. It is designed to keep the simulation running indefinitely by preventing total stock depletion, creating a "closed-loop" economic system.

Logic Flow: This rule triggers specifically when an inventory item is tagged with the needsRestock property (which was generated by Rule #2).

Execution: It calculates the deficit required to return the stock to its maximumCapacity. It then updates the hasStockQuantity to this maximum value.

Impact: This rule provides the system with self-healing capabilities. Even if customer demand spikes unexpectedly, the system automatically detects the shortage and replenishes resources, allowing the market simulation to continue without human intervention or system crashes.
